language.name: Eldarindëva
language.description: The living tongue shared across all branches of the Eldarindë, from the Taralindë of the High Realm to the Sylvarindë of the deep forests. Warm, vowel-rich, and melodic, with a flowing cadence suited to song, poetry, and formal address. Distinguished from ancient forms by softer consonant clusters and a preference for open syllables. Regional dialects exist among the wood elves and drow, but the written standard remains unified.
language.note: Names of the Elyndarûm (the divine pantheon) are sacred names that exist outside the normal linguistic rules and should not be derived from or decomposed into standard roots.

consonants.description: Always hard, as in 'cat'. Never soft.
As in 'dawn'. Only appears word-initially or after n.
Always hard, as in 'gift'. Never soft.
Soft aspiration, as in 'home'. Silent between identical vowels.
Always clear and light, as in 'lily'. Never dark.
Light single tap, as in Spanish 'pero'. Never harsh or trilled.
Light and dental, as in French 'tu'.
As in 'yes'. Consonantal only before vowels.
Palatalized l, as in Italian 'figlio'. Rare, poetic.

vowels.description: Open, as in 'father'. The most common vowel.
Open-mid, as in 'bed'. Clear and warm.
Always pronounced as a distinct syllable, never silent. Used word-finally.
Open-mid, as in 'for'. Round and full.
Long u, held longer. Rare, used in sacred or ancient words.

diphthongs.description: Falling, as in 'aisle'. Very common, the signature Arandori sound.
Two syllables flowing together. As in 'idea' but smoother.

phonotacticRules.description: Every syllable contains exactly one vowel or diphthong. Initial consonant is optional. Final consonant is optional but limited to: n, r, l, s, th.
Stress falls on the second-to-last syllable. Words ending in -ë shift stress to the third-to-last syllable. Monosyllabic words are stressed.
Only these consonant clusters may begin a syllable. All other syllables begin with a single consonant or a vowel.
Syllables (and words) may only end in these consonants, or in a vowel. Most words end in vowels.
These sounds or sequences do not exist in Arandori. The sounds x, j (as in 'judge'), w, and z never appear.
Within a single root, front vowels (e, i) and back vowels (o, u) tend not to mix. The vowel 'a' is neutral and combines freely with any vowel. Compound words may cross harmony boundaries at the join.
When a voiceless consonant (t, s, th) meets a voiced consonant (d, v) at a compound boundary, the voiceless softens: t+v becomes 'lv', s+d becomes 'nd'.
Most Arandori words end in a vowel. Masculine-coded words often end in -on, -or, -ar, -en. Feminine-coded words often end in -ë, -a, -iel, -iel. Neuter words end in -in, -il, -al.

wordOrder.description: Everyday speech uses Verb-Subject-Object. Formal address, law, and military commands use Subject-Object-Verb. Poetry allows free order since noun cases prevent ambiguity.

articles.description: 'I' (the) precedes the noun. 'Na' (a/an) precedes the noun. Omitted in poetry and proper nouns.

nounCases.description: Subject of the sentence. Unmarked base form.

nounCases.example: aranor = of the king / the king's

plurals.description: Standard plural adds -i (aran > arani, kings). Collective -ath denotes 'all of a kind' (elenath, all the stars). Dual -at for exactly two (aranat, two kings).
rules: Words ending in -a change to -ai (fanya > fanyai)
Words ending in -ë change to -i (dorë > dori)
Words ending in a consonant add -i (elen > eleni)

adjectives.agreement: Adjectives agree with noun number but not case.
adjectives.formation: Many adjectives formed with suffix -ea (morëa = shadowy, from mor = shadow) or -in (valin = eternal, from val = enduring).

verbs.negation: Prefix ú- before the verb: útirna = does not watch.

compoundFormation.description: The modifying element comes first. 'Star-shield' = ael (star) + thalon (shield) = aelthalon. At compound boundaries, consonant clusters may simplify per phonotactic rules.
joiningRules: If the first root ends in a consonant and the second begins with one, insert linking vowel -a- or -i-: thal + nor = thalanor
If both elements end/begin with vowels, the first vowel may elide: aeli + elen = aelelen or aelelen
The suffix -ion/-arion denotes 'son of / bearer of': calarion = light-bearer
The suffix -iel/-ithiel denotes 'daughter of / maiden of': eleniel = star-maiden
The suffix -ndor/-dor denotes 'land of / realm of': arandor = high-realm
The suffix -rindë denotes 'kindred / people / folk': eldarindë = elf-kindred, sylvarindë = forest-kindred
The suffix -va/-ëva denotes 'speech of / pertaining to / of the nature of': Eldarindëva = speech of the elven-kind
The suffix -ath denotes collective totality: elenath = all the stars, eldarionath = all noble elves

pronouns.note: Often dropped; verb suffix implies subject

entries.etymology: Primordial root. One of the oldest words in the language.
Primordial root associated with visible light and divine illumination.
Primordial root. Connotes active defense rather than passive shelter.
Primordial root. Neutral connotation; shadow is not inherently evil in Arandori thought, merely the complement of light.
Primordial root associated with moonlight and reflected starlight.
Primordial root. Carries connotations of both temporal permanence and spiritual sanctity.
Primordial root. The Arandori do not distinguish between the sound of flowing water and music.
Extended form of ael with individualizing suffix -en.
From ancient root dor- (settled place) + nominalizing -ë.
From ara- (noble, high) + -n (agent). One who is noble.
Primordial root. Implies watchful protection rather than aggressive defense.
From van- (fair, blessed) + -ye (abstract quality).
Onomatopoeic origin, evoking the soft sound of cloth or mist moving.
From er- (lone, singular) + -yn (quality). Originally 'singular in ability.'
Primordial root. Used as both standalone adjective and productive prefix.
Primordial root. Distinct from dorë (realm); nor is the physical earth itself.
Primordial root associated with both physical and spiritual might.
Primordial root. Carries connotation of immensity and depth.
From nau- (heat, warmth) + -r (intensifier).
Used as collective suffix: elenath = all the stars.
From rim- (outer) + -a (nominalizer).
Ancient compound os- (wall) + to- (stand). A thing that stands walled.
From mi- (in, within) + na- (upward). That which rises from within.
From thal (protection) + -on (concrete object suffix).
From cal (light) + -a (noun form). The tangible presence of light.
From mor (shadow) + -ë (noun form).
From sil (silver) + -më (emanation, quality that radiates).
From ael (star) + lin (water). Star-water: where stars are reflected.
From lin (song) + dalë (craft, making).
Root dal- (to shape, to make) + -ë.
aran (king/high) + dorë (realm). The name of the elvish civilization itself.
elda (elf, star-person) + -inë (collective people).
ael (star) + lin (song/linking) + dor (protector). A shield that sings with starlight.
cal (light) + -arion (bearer/carrier).
vael (wind) + lis (keen) + -ar (instrument).
eryn (swift) + dor (one who acts).
tir (to watch) + -ion (great/mighty thing).
sil (silver) + var (eternal) + -on (entity).
aer (sky/upper air) + an (toward) + dor (lord/ruler).

definitions: name of a stealth frigate class
entries.etymology: thal (shield) + anis (silence, stillness).

definitions: name of a light frigate class
entries.etymology: thalas (sea-shield, from thal + uin) + rën (light/lesser).
tal (to carry, to bear) + asir (faithfully, steadily).
var (sacred/eternal) + dal (craft) + -um (substance). Literally: eternally-crafted substance.
From elen (star) + -da (being). A being of starlight. The base word for all elvenkind.
From rin- (to join, to gather) + -dë (collective noun). Used as a suffix to form the names of elvish peoples and houses.
elda (elf) + rindë (kindred). All those who share elven blood. Also the root of the language name: Eldarindëva (speech of the elven-kind).
From tar- (high, lofty, crowned) + -a. Distinct from ara (noble); tara implies formal sovereignty and the right to rule.
tara (crowned/sovereign) + lindë (from lin, song + rindë, kindred, contracted). The elves who bear the crown. The formal name of the High Elven race.
cal (light) + var (eternal) + rindë (kindred). The eternally radiant kindred. A poetic alternative name for the High Elves.
elda (elf) + arion (noble bearer) + -ath (collective). All the noble-born elves. A formal, archaic name for the High Elves.
From sil- (gleam) shifting to syl- (green-gleam) + -va (domain). The shimmering green depths of ancient forest.
sylva (forest) + rindë (kindred). The kindred of the deep forest. The formal name of the Wood Elven race.
From um- (beneath) + -bra (darkness, intensified mor). A deeper, more absolute darkness than morë.
umbra (deep shadow) + rindë (kindred). The kindred who dwell in deep shadow. The formal name of the Drow.
aurë (day/dawn) + -va (pertaining to). Used in the compound Aurë-elves: elves of the dawn, keepers of ancient knowledge.
From ael (star) with consonant shift l>r denoting the medium rather than the point of light.
From va- (movement) + ael (sky). The sky in motion.
From tau- (tall, growing) + -r (place).
Extended form of uin with poetic vowel expansion.
Onomatopoeic: the soft opening of a petal.
From gal- (to grow) + -adh (living entity).
From ith- (pale) + sil (silver). The silver pale one.
From an- (golden/warm) + -or (great one).
Primordial root for water in its flowing, drinkable form.
Onomatopoeic: the sound of stone struck.
Primordial root. The animating essence of a living being.
Primordial root. The material housing of the fëa.
From oi- (ever) + -alë (state of being).
An elvish measure of time, far longer than a mortal year.
From au- (sun, warmth) + -rë (period).
From lóm- (twilight) + -ë.
From anor (sun) + -rë (moment/event). The sun's event.
From an (toward) + dún (west/setting) + -ë.
From her- (to rule) + -u (agent).
Feminine form of heru.
amba- (highest) + aran (king). Established AEN rank.
elda (star-person) + mar (home/command). Established AEN rank.
rimba (fleet, many ships) + -len (master). Established AEN rank.
rimba (fleet) + -aer (lesser/subordinate). Established AEN rank.

thal (shield) + -ion (great one). One who is a great shield.
From oht- (war) + -ar (agent).
Ancient root cir- (to cut through) + -ya (thing). That which cuts the void.
From rim- (many, numerous) + -ba (collective of vessels).
From mac- (to cut) + -il (instrument).
From eht- (to pierce) + -ar (instrument).
Onomatopoeic: the sound of a bowstring.
Primordial root for love in its deepest sense.
mel (love) + -da (state of being).
From al- (great) + cal (light) + -ar. Great radiance.
From es- (to begin) + tel (end/purpose). Trust that the beginning leads to a worthy end.
From fai- (fade) + -rë (entity).
From sér- (to rest, to be still) + -ë.
From nol- (to know) + -më (abstract quality).
From sai- (to understand deeply) + -la (having the quality of).

mor (shadow) + -ëa (adjectival, 'having the quality of').
cal (light) + -ima (capable of, full of).
From tul- (to stand firm) + -ca (quality).
From hos- (to collect) + -ta (verbal).

From ran- (to flow, to move freely) + -ya (verbal).
From cen- (to perceive) + -ta (verbal). To make perceived.

From lin (song) + -da (verbal action).
From or (upon/above) + -ta (verbal).
From lan- (down) + -ta (verbal).
From tul- (to come) + -ya (causative). To cause to come.
From has- (to cleave through) + -ta (verbal). Originally 'to cut through water,' now applied to void-travel.
From lui- (deep) + -na (colored). Established in month name Luinavas.
From sil (silver) + quen (bright). Established in month name Silquendil.
From lis- (sweet) + -ë (quality). Established in month name Lissuinion.
From ur- (fire/heat) + ith (sky) + -ë. Established in month name Urithiel.
From naur (fire) + -vion (diminishing). Established in month name Narviondë.
From myn- (memory) + dar (to linger). Established in month name Myndarial.

From mor (shadow) + il (light) + thë (state). Established in month name Morilthië.
From al- (to grow tall) + -da (living thing).
From lai- (green/growing) + -rë (season).
From hrív- (chill) + -ë.
From yav- (fruit, harvest) + -ië (season).
From tui- (to sprout) + -li (beginning).
Primordial root.
Primordial root. Applied to any object of great value.
From cui- (to awaken) + -vë (event/moment).
Primordial root.
From oth- (outer) + -ar (person).
From nil- (friend) + -më (abstract quality).
From nil- (friend) + -dë (person).
From ves- (to bind) + -ta (act). A binding act.
From her- (to rule) + -ë (abstract).
From cán- (to order/command) + -o (agent).

definitions: one newly sworn to the fleet
entries.etymology: From gwer- (to begin, to set out) + -eth (novice). AEN Navy rank E-1.
From tûr- (mastery, skill) + wen (young person). AEN Navy rank E-2.
From mae- (skilled) + -gor (hand/doer). A skilled hand. AEN Navy rank E-3.
From car- (to do, to make) + -n (agent). One who does. AEN Navy rank E-4.
From rath- (to course, to set in order). One who keeps order. AEN Navy rank E-5.
From ber- (valor) + -eth (one who holds). One who holds valor. AEN Navy rank E-6.
From naur (fire) + -on (great one). A keeper of the flame of duty. AEN Navy rank E-7.
From dûr- (deep, enduring) + -n (agent). One of deep experience. AEN Navy rank E-8.
From ael (star) + -gor (hand/doer). A hand guided by stars. AEN Navy rank E-9.
From ael (star) + aran (noble) contracted. Born under the stars of command. AEN Navy rank O-1.
From elen (star) + -or (of). Of the stars. AEN Navy rank O-2.
From fae- (spirit, fairness) + -l (agent). One of fair spirit. AEN Navy rank O-3.
From mith- (grey, experienced) + rand (wanderer/voyager). AEN Navy rank O-9.
From aran (king) + -dur (servant/steward). The king's steward of the void. AEN Navy rank O-12.
From cau- (to begin anew) + -r (agent). One who begins. AEN Marine rank E-1.
caur (recruit) + -en (proven). A recruit who has been tested. AEN Marine rank E-2.
From hol- (to close, to seal) + -len (one who). One who closes ranks. AEN Marine rank E-3.
From bran- (to endure, to hold fast) + -non (person). AEN Marine rank E-4.
From thal (shield) + -nd (standing). One who stands as a shield. AEN Marine rank E-5.
From ron- (to arch, to span) + -d (solid). A solid arch. AEN Marine rank E-6.
From mab- (hand, grasp) + ur- (fire). Hand of fire. AEN Marine rank E-7.
From vor- (ever-faithful, steadfast) + -on (one). The ever-faithful one. AEN Marine rank E-8.
From gar- (to hold, to possess) + aran (lord). One who holds lordship among soldiers. AEN Marine rank E-9.
From var (eternal/sacred) + -eth (one who holds). One who holds the eternal watch. AEN Marine rank E-10.
From cal (light) + -ar (agent). Bearer of specialized light. AEN Marine rank O-1W.
From nim- (white, pale, new). A fresh spark of command. AEN Marine rank O-1.
From ril- (to glitter, to gleam). AEN Marine rank O-2.
From gal- (to shine, radiance) + -ad (standing). Shining in place. AEN Marine rank O-3.
From dor (land/realm) + -en (of). One of the realm. AEN Marine rank O-4.
From cer- (to encircle, to fortify) + -in (quality). One who fortifies. AEN Marine rank O-5.
From eor- (lone, solitary peak) + -th (standing). A lone mountain of authority. AEN Marine rank O-6.
From thel- (purpose, intent) + -ion (great one). One of great purpose. AEN Marine rank O-7.
From or- (above, upon) + -on (great). A great height. AEN Marine rank O-8.
From am- (upward) + -on (place). A high place of command. AEN Marine rank O-9.
From ara (noble/high) + -ath (collective). The height of all nobility. AEN Marine rank O-10.
From val (mighty) + nor (land). Mighty lord of all the land. AEN Marine rank O-11.
Primordial copula. The simplest assertion of existence.
From an- (toward) + -ta (verbal). To move toward another.
From map- (to grasp) + -a (verbal).
From les- (to move onward) + -ta (verbal).
From tul- (to come) + -a (verbal). Base of tulya (to lead, causative).
From cen- (to perceive) + -a (verbal). Broader than tir (to watch/guard).
From las- (ear, to listen) + -ta (verbal).
From har- (to sit, to settle) + -a (verbal).

From yes- (first, initial) + -ta (verbal).
From nur- (deep, hidden) + -ta (verbal).
From rauc- (to crack) + -a (verbal).
From pan- (to spread wide) + -ta (verbal).

From col- (to bear) + -a (verbal). Related to talasir (burden-bearer).
From lor- (to dream, to slumber) + -na (verbal).
From cui- (to awaken) + -va (verbal). Related to cuivë (awakening).
From yal- (to cry, to summon) + -la (verbal).
From es- (being, identity) + -ta (verbal). To give essence.
From en- (re-, again) + vanya (to go). To go again.
From naur (fire) + -a (verbal). To be as fire.
From vil- (air, flight) + -ya (verbal).
From tan- (to forge, to fashion) + -a (verbal). Related to tanar (war-captain, one who forges).
From fir- (to fade away) + -ia (verbal). The gentle Arandori term for death.
From sin- (to go steadily) + -na (verbal).
From nor (land) + -ta (verbal). To course across the land.
From cal (light) + -ta (verbal). To produce light.

Primordial root for the female of the species.

From mas- (to knead, to prepare) + -sa (result).

From ser- (to flow) + -cë (substance).

Primordial root. The physical and spiritual center.
From cas- (top, crest).

From cov- (to shelter) + -a (place).
From ham- (to clothe) + -ma (material). The clothing of war.

From san- (to affirm, to know surely) + -da (quality).
From fir- (to fade) + -in (state). Having faded from life.
From li- (many, multiple) + -ë.
From sin- (small) + -omë (measure).
From dín- (silence) + -a (quality).
From er- (one, lone) + -essë (in state of). In the state of being one.
From ain- (before all) + -ëa (quality). Older than yára; predating memory itself.
From laurë (gold, golden light) + -a (adjectival).
From cú (bow) + -na (quality). Having the quality of a bow.
From mal- (gold as material) + -da (made of).
From nor- (hard, knotted) + -na (quality).
From lar- (to rush) + -ca (quality). Swifter than eryn; implies urgency.
From yan- (to join, to connect) + -da (structure). That which joins two sides.
Primordial root. A word spoken that binds the soul.
From sérë (peace) + vesta (oath). A peace sealed by oath.
From centa (speech) + -ar (agent). One who speaks on behalf of a realm.

From hérë (authority) + mar (house). The house of authority.
From hérë (authority) + -ath (collective). Any formal gathering that holds collective authority. The generic term; contrast with Valarindë for the specific supreme council.

definitions: the Council of the Seven Great Houses
supreme governing council of the Arandorë Eldainë
entries.etymology: From val (mighty/divine) + rindë (kindred/folk). The mighty kindred; the seven ruling houses gathered as one voice. Proper noun: always capitalized as 'the Valarindë' on subsequent reference.
From tara (high/royal) + halla (tall/lofty). The exalted seat.
From cáno (commander) + -va (pertaining to). That which pertains to command.
From san- (to arrange, to order) + -yar (established thing). An established ordering.
From san- (to arrange) + -no (agent). One who arranges what is just.
From han- (to understand, to feel for) + -yalë (abstract quality). The quality of compassionate understanding.
From ranya (to wander) + -ar (agent). One who wanders for commerce.
From mir (precious/jewel) + -lë (abstract). A precious offering.
From var (sacred/eternal) + -ro (agent). One who serves the sacred.
From var (sacred) + -riel (feminine agent). A woman who serves the sacred.

definitions: restorer of peace to the body
entries.etymology: From sérë (peace/rest) + -no (agent). One who brings peace to the afflicted.
From nolmë (knowledge) + -tar (high agent). A high keeper of lore.

definitions: one who watches what shall be

From man- (to bind) + -dos (enclosed place). A place of binding.

From tara (high/royal) + corma (ring). The royal ring worn upon the brow.
From mir (precious) + -ien (small thing). A small precious thing.
From panta (to open) + cor (round). The round thing that opens.
From asc- (hard inner) + -a (thing).
From hel- (to cover) + -vë (surface).
From thúl- (to breathe) + -ë (thing). Related to fëa (soul) through breath.
From nír- (to weep) + -ë (thing).
From olor- (to dream) + -ë (thing). A vision of sleep.
From naic- (to bite, to sting) + -ë (thing).
From sang- (to corrupt) + -wa (liquid).
From sérë (peace) + lossë (blossom). A blossom that brings peace to the body.
From ros- (to scatter from above) + -së (substance).
From raum- (to rage) + -o (entity). A raging entity.
From vael (wind) + raumo (storm). The storm's wind-voice.
From cal (light) + raumo (storm). The light of the storm.
From los- (white, pure) + -së (substance). Pure white substance.
From hel- (cold, frozen) + -cë (substance).
From lith- (grey remnant) + -i (thing).
From usqu- (to rise darkly) + -ë (thing).
From tol- (to stand alone) + -lë (place). A place that stands alone.
From imb- (between, in the gap) + -a (place).
From falas- (shore, coast) + -së (formation).
From hrót- (to hollow) + -a (place). A hollowed place.
From an- (to be truly) + -wa (state). The state of being real.
From fui- (to twist, to distort) + -lë (thing).
From nó- (deep, inner) + -lë (thing). A deeply hidden thing.
Primordial root. The weight of what must be.
From ler- (to release, to free) + -ya (state).
From moi- (to labor under) + -a (state).
From ner- (to do what is required) + -më (abstract).
From anta (to give) + -vë (act of). The act of giving fully.
From envanya (to return) + -lë (abstract). The quality of returning.
From aic- (sharp, keen) + -alë (abstract pursuit). The sharp pursuit of justice.
From han- (to understand) + -yassë (state of grace). The grace of understanding.
From har- (to sit, to remain) + -më (quality). The quality of remaining.
From ohtar (warrior) + cárë (making). The making of endless conflict.
From san- (to arrange) + -yalë (abstract quality). The quality of right arrangement.
From rai- (to reach beyond) + -në (thing). A thing beyond reach.
From al- (great) + mar (self/home) + -éa (state). The state of seeing oneself as great.
From nú- (beneath) + -tëa (state). The state of placing oneself beneath.
From is- (to know) + -ta (verbal). Deeper than cena (to see); knowing with the mind.
From tec- (to mark) + -a (verbal).
From cen- (to perceive) + -ta (verbal causative). To cause another to perceive.
From fas- (to weave) + -ta (verbal).
From or- (above, upward) + -ta (verbal). To move upward.
From cai- (to lay down) + -ta (verbal).
From ler- (to release) + -ya (verbal).
From tul- (to lead) + -ta (causative). To lead another to knowledge.
From par- (to arrange, compose) + -ya (verbal).
From ais- (holy breath) + -ta (verbal).
From ruh- (to darken upon) + -ta (verbal).
From ava- (away from) + tyar- (cause). To cause blame to go away.
From en- (re-, again) + vanya (to go). To go again.
From nam- (to weigh, to measure) + -ma (verbal).

definitions: to walk in the steps of
entries.etymology: From hil- (to follow) + -ya (verbal).
Primordial root. To move from here to there.
From nol- (knowledge) + -ya (verbal). To acquire knowing.
From lin (song) + -da (verbal). To produce song.
From hos- (to heap, to bring together) + -ta (verbal).
From rin- (to recall) + -ta (verbal).
From hes- (to single out) + -ta (verbal).
From tara (high) + -ya (verbal). To make oneself the highest.
From lanta (to fall) + -lda (willingly). To fall of one's own will.
From taracorma (crown) + -ya (verbal). To bestow the crown.
From nírë (tear) + -ya (verbal). To produce tears.
From nos- (kin, clan-root) + -të (formal collective). A formal kinship group with political standing. Distinct from mar (dwelling).

definitions: one of the seven ruling houses
entries.etymology: From ara (high/noble) + nostë (house). A house of the highest rank.
From hên (child) + -ion (great one/bearer). The child who bears the mantle.
From nos- (kin) + sírë (flowing line). The flowing line of kinship.
From nostë (house) + sercë (blood) + -ion (great). The great blood-house.
From nor (land) + mar (dwelling). The land-dwelling; the totality of a house's domain.

definitions: one who shelters beneath another's shield
entries.etymology: From nur- (beneath) + thal (shield). One who stands beneath the shield of a lord.
From thal (shield) + tir (to watch/guard). The shield-watcher; the one who guards from above.
From tara (high/royal) + -no (agent). One who attends the royal.
From mar (house) + tir (to guard). The guardian of the house.
From hérë (authority) + cáno (commander). The commander of the realm's governance.
From nor (land) + tir (to guard). The guardian of the land.
From anta (to give) + -më (abstract). The act of giving across generations.
From nossírë (lineage) + cár (making/course). The course of lineage.
From yesta (to begin) + nermë (duty). The duty that begins at birth.

definitions: heraldic device of a noble house
entries.etymology: From nostë (house) + calma (light-mark). The light-mark of a house; the emblem that identifies.
From ranel (banner) + -lië (art/collection). The art and system of noble identification.

definitions: the pull of a body upon another
entries.etymology: From lung- (heavy, to bear down) + -ë (thing). The quality of bearing down. The Arandori understanding of gravitational attraction.
From lungë (weight/pull) + sanyar (law). The law of weight. The formal term for gravitational theory in Arandori natural philosophy.

definitions: that which causes motion or change
entries.etymology: From tulca (strong/firm) + -lë (abstract quality). The abstract quality of strength applied; any cause of change in motion.
From cuivë (awakening/life) + -lë (quality). The life-quality; that which awakens or moves things. Encompasses kinetic, thermal, and radiant energy.

definitions: the measure of matter in a body
entries.etymology: From ondo (stone/material) + -lë (measure). The stone-measure; how much substance a thing contains. Distinguished from lungë (weight) which depends on context.
From ondo (stone) + hosta (to gather). How tightly the substance is gathered.

definitions: the curved track a body follows around another
entries.etymology: From tir (to watch/circle) + -iel (daughter/thing born of). The path born of watching; the path a lesser body traces around a greater. Named for the observation that orbits were first understood by watching.
From quer- (to turn) + -ië (continuous action). The continuous turning.

definitions: the rhythmic rise governed by celestial bodies
entries.etymology: From uin (great water) + -lië (rhythmic pattern). The rhythmic pattern of the great waters.
From sír- (to flow in a direction) + -ë (thing). A directed flow; applies to water, air, and energy.
From fal- (to foam, to crest) + -ma (thing). A cresting thing; applies to ocean waves, sound, and light.
From falma (wave) + querië (rotation/turning). How often the wave turns back upon itself.

definitions: the full range of light separated into its parts
entries.etymology: From cala (light) + cárië (spreading/making). The spreading of light; when white light is separated into its constituent colors.
From mor (darkness) + vael (air/sky) + -ë (place). The dark sky; the space between celestial bodies. Not simply darkness but the medium (or lack thereof) through which light and ships travel.

definitions: cloud of luminous gas from which stars are born
entries.etymology: From fanya (cloud/mist) + calë (light). A luminous cloud. The stellar nurseries.

definitions: a point of infinite density from which no light returns
entries.etymology: From lungë (gravity/weight) + tumna (deep/profound). The deepest gravity; the point beyond which substance collapses past understanding.

definitions: one of the irreducible kinds of matter
entries.etymology: From er- (single, alone) + -thë (essence). A single essence; a substance that cannot be broken into simpler parts.

definitions: the smallest particle of an element
entries.etymology: From erthë (element) + -in (diminutive). The smallest grain of an element.

definitions: anything that has mass and occupies space
entries.etymology: From ondo (stone/material) + -assë (general category). The general category of material; all that is substantial.
From sanmë (thought) + tírië (watching/observation). A thought built from watching; a model of understanding tested against what is observed.

definitions: an invariant principle observed in nature
entries.etymology: From erthë (element/fundamental) + sanyar (law). A fundamental law; a principle that holds without exception across all observation.

definitions: a deliberate watching under controlled conditions
entries.etymology: From centa (to speak/declare) + tírië (watching). A watching that speaks; an observation designed to answer a question.
From tir (to watch) + -ië (continuous act). The continuous act of watching.

definitions: the act of assigning number to observation
entries.etymology: From not- (to count, to reckon) + -iër (process). The process of counting.
From not- (to count) + -ië (art/practice). The practice of counting; mathematics.
From hen (eye) + calassë (glass). Eye-glass; a shaped transparent medium.

definitions: instrument for observing distant celestial bodies
entries.etymology: From ael (star) + tírion (place of watching). The star-watching instrument.
From tië (path) + hir (to find) + -ë (thing). The path-finding thing.
From ista (to know) + calma (device/mark). A device for knowing; any scientific instrument.

definitions: directed flow of energy through space
entries.etymology: From cala (light) + sírë (current). A current of light; any electromagnetic radiation.

definitions: a disturbance in the fabric of space caused by massive bodies
entries.etymology: From lungë (gravity) + falma (wave). A wave of gravity; predicted by Arandori field theory and observed by sensitive instruments.

definitions: the woven structure of space and time through which all things move
entries.etymology: From morvailë (void/deep space) + fassë (cloth/fabric). The fabric of the void; the unified structure of space and time.

commonPhrases.meaning: A star shines upon the hour of our meeting (formal greeting)
May the sacred light guard you (blessing/dismissal)
What do you say? / What is your report?
Set course for the stars (naval command to depart)
Glory to the High Realm (patriotic/military salute)
The soul finds its still water (proverb about inner peace)
